meta_title: For Students: freelance pitching to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Freelance Pitching to Solve 'No Project Ideas'
content.seo_description: Learn how to use freelance pitching to overcome 'no project ideas'. This intermediate case study uses Antigravity and Claude Code to build your portfolio.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for inspiration. The fastest way to build a portfolio is to solve real-world problems for local businesses. By mastering freelance pitching, you turn their operational pain points into your technical milestones. Use Antigravity to structure your workflow and Claude Code to accelerate development, moving from blank screen to production-ready solution.
content.intro:
The screen is a void. You have the technical skills, the syntax memorized, and the environment configured, but the 'blank canvas' paralysis is absolute. You aren't lacking ability; you are lacking constraints. Without a real user or a genuine business problem, your code remains an academic exercise that fails to impress recruiters or clients.

Real-world development isn't about inventing the next unicorn—it is about shipping solutions for people who have money and problems. By shifting your focus from 'what should I build?' to 'what do they need?', you unlock a pipeline of professional-grade projects that prove your value. This is how you stop being a student and start being an engineer.

steps.title: Identify a High-Value Niche
steps.description: Select a local industry like real estate or independent gyms. Find a repetitive, manual process they currently manage on paper or spreadsheets.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a business consultant. Identify 5 common operational bottlenecks for small independent gyms that can be solved with a web-based automated dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude research --topic 'common operational software pain points for small gym owners'

steps.title: Craft the Value-Based Pitch
steps.description: Write a pitch that focuses on ROI rather than technical specs. Frame your project as a tool that saves them 5 hours a week.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a cold email for a gym owner. Focus on reducing administrative overhead. Keep it under 150 words and end with a soft call to action.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --file pitch_draft.txt --content 'Draft a concise email offering to build a custom automation tool for gym scheduling.'

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Use technical scoping to ensure the project is buildable within two weeks. Avoid feature creep by focusing on one core transformation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a technical project requirements document for a gym scheduling MVP. Include database schema for users, classes, and bookings.

steps.title: Rapid Iteration with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scaffold the backend and database integration. Focus on clean architecture from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a boilerplate structure for a Next.js and Supabase application optimized for rapid CRUD operations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project with Next.js and Supabase integration based on the schema provided'

steps.title: Deployment and Feedback Loop
steps.description: Deploy to Vercel and present the prototype to your client. Use their feedback to refine your build, turning the project into a case study.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a post-project reflection template that highlights the technical challenges solved and the business impact achieved.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --command 'npm run build && vercel deploy'

pro_tips: Always pitch a 'paid pilot' rather than free work; it forces the client to take the project seriously.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write it; this makes your portfolio repo look 10x more professional.
Focus on one single 'pain point' instead of building a full suite; niche tools are easier to sell and faster to build.
Record a Loom video walk-through of your prototype before sending the final link to the client.
Include a 'system architecture' diagram in your README; recruiters value system design over raw code snippets.
content.next_steps: After completing your first build, document the results in a GitHub repository README, record a screen-share video, and reach out to three more businesses in the same industry to scale your solution.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to turn cold leads into high-value portfolio pieces. Stop building toy apps and start shipping real solutions that get you hired. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
